title: Two Sum — Find Pair with Target
description:
Given an array of N integers and a target sum T, find and print the indices (0-based) of two numbers that add up to T. Print them in ascending order separated by a space. It is guaranteed that exactly one valid pair exists.

Input Format:
- Line 1: Two integers N and T
- Line 2: N space-separated integers

Constraints:
- 2 <= N <= 10^5
- -10^9 <= arr[i] <= 10^9

Output Format:
- Two space-separated indices in ascending order

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description:
Given a string S, find and print the longest substring of S that is a palindrome. If there are multiple palindromic substrings of the same maximum length, print the one that appears first.

Input Format:
- Line 1: A single string S (lowercase English letters only)

Constraints:
- 1 <= |S| <= 1000

Output Format:
- The longest palindromic substring

title: Reverse a Singly Linked List
description:
Given a singly linked list of N integers, reverse it and print the reversed list.

Input Format:
- Line 1: An integer N (number of nodes)
- Line 2: N space-separated integers representing node values

Constraints:
- 0 <= N <= 10^5
- -10^4 <= node value <= 10^4

Output Format:
- The reversed linked list values separated by spaces. Print "empty" if the list is empty.

title: Binary Tree Inorder Traversal
description:
Given a binary tree represented as a level-order array (using -1 for null nodes), print its inorder traversal (Left, Root, Right).

Input Format:
- Line 1: An integer N (number of elements in level-order, including -1 for nulls)
- Line 2: N space-separated integers (-1 represents a null node)

Constraints:
- 1 <= N <= 1000
- Node values are between -100 and 100 (excluding -1 which means null)

Output Format:
- Space-separated values of the inorder traversal

title: Climbing Stairs — Dynamic Programming
description:
You are climbing a staircase. It takes N steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. Print the number of distinct ways you can climb to the top.

Input Format:
- Line 1: A single integer N

Constraints:
- 1 <= N <= 45

Output Format:
- A single integer representing the number of distinct ways
